_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Sunday March 1st | 5:44 

Bonjour Tremblant,

Winter has made a bold return to Mont Tremblant today, with summit temperatures reaching a brisk -17°C! It is a true mid-season feel out there, so be sure to bundle up and layer properly before heading out. The reward is a stunning sunny day with light to moderate winds, creating classic bluebird conditions; it is the kind of day that reminds us why we love winter in the Laurentians! 

Moreover, we have some good news! Our teams, supported by our specialized partners and Doppelmayr, have completed all required steps and the Duncan Express lift is back in operation as of today, with an expected opening at 8:30 a.m. We are also hosting the First Tracks event for IKON Pass holders this morning, offering exclusive mountain access starting at 7:30 a.m. Early laps will be available via Expo and Lowell on the North Side, as well as the Télécabine and Soleil lifts.

Please note that the touring trails are currently on a hardened base due to the weather conditions of the past few days. Be sure to exercise caution while out on the trails. 

Bundle up, soak in the sunshine, and take advantage of this beauty of a winter day! 
It is shaping up to be a fantastic day on the mountain!

Mountain safety tip of the week: Ski and Ride with Caution.
Skiers and snowboarders should be aware that trail difficulty ratings (green circle, blue square, black diamond, and double black diamond) are not necessarily the same from one mountain to another. The rating system is relative and applies only to this mountain. When visiting a new mountain, skiers and riders of all ability levels should start on the easiest trails and gradually progress as they become familiar with the terrain.

Plan Your Runs
  • Choose trails that match your ability level.
  • Accidents can occur when skiers and snowboarders find themselves on terrain beyond their skill level. Before heading down the mountain, plan your route using a trail map.
Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Happy Sunday Tremblant! Summit temperatures reach -17°C today under sunny skies with light to moderate winds. The Duncan Express lift reopens at 8:30 a.m. after work completed by our teams and Doppelmayr. The First Tracks event for IKON Pass holders begins at 7:30 a.m., with early access via Expo and Lowell on the North Side, and Télécabine and Soleil. Dress warmly and enjoy this beauty of a winter day!
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Bon dimanche Tremblant! La température au sommet atteindra -17°C sous un ciel ensoleillé avec des vents modérés. La remontée Duncan Express ouvrira à 8 h 30 après les travaux complétés par nos équipes et Doppelmayr. L’événement Premières Traces pour les détenteurs de la IKON Pass débute à 7 h 30, avec accès via Expo et Lowell au Versant Nord, ainsi que la Télécabine et Soleil. Habillez-vous chaudement et profitez de cette journée hivernale!
